sections.title: Leadership
sections.summary: CEO Daniel S. Letter and Executive Chairman Hamid R. Moghadam lead a REIT that generated $8.02 billion in FY2025 revenue and signed a company-record 228 million square feet of leases. CFO Timothy D. Arndt oversees a balance sheet with $53.95 billion in stockholders' equity and A2/A credit ratings.
points: Daniel S. Letter serves as CEO and Director, signing the 10-K on February 13, 2026, while Hamid R. Moghadam serves as Executive Chairman, providing continuity since the AMB-ProLogis merger 14 years ago.

The leadership team delivered same store NOI growth of 4.5% on a net effective basis for full year 2025, with same store portfolio NOI of approximately $8.95 billion across roughly 1,125 million square feet.

sections.title: Capital Allocation
sections.summary: Prologis maintains a diversified debt structure with maturities extending to 2054 and debt at 24.6% of its $160.9 billion gross market capitalization. The company manages eight unconsolidated co-investment ventures spanning the US, Europe, Japan, China, and Brazil, leveraging partner capital alongside its own.
points: The company holds A2/A credit ratings from Moody's and S&P, supported by a diversified debt structure including senior notes in USD, EUR, GBP, and CAD, yen-denominated term loans, and a global credit facility amended in May 2025.

Prologis operates its Strategic Capital segment through co-investment vehicles such as Prologis US Logistics Venture, Prologis European Logistics Partners Holding, and Prologis Japan Core Logistics Fund, amplifying asset management reach without proportionate balance sheet exposure.

sections.title: Shareholder Alignment
sections.summary: Prologis' annualized TSR since the AMB-ProLogis merger outperformed the MSCI U.S. REIT Index by 561 bps per year and the S&P 500 Equal Weighted Index by 102 bps. Compensation design ties executive pay to relative TSR outperformance through forward-looking PSU awards and the Prologis Promote Plan.
points: The compensation program includes annual bonus opportunities, long-term incentive equity awards, and the Prologis Promote Plan as outperformance compensation, with PSU awards specifically incentivizing relative TSR outperformance.
Sustainability metrics comprise 10% of the overall corporate bonus scorecard, aligning executive incentives with Global Impact & Sustainability performance overseen by the Governance Committee.

sections.title: Communication
sections.summary: Prologis reports through two segments-Real Estate Operations and Strategic Capital-with geographic breakdowns across the US, Other Americas, Europe, and Asia in its 10-K filed February 13, 2026. The DEF 14A filed March 19, 2026 provides detailed operational metrics including same store NOI, lease volume, and capitalization data.
points: The 10-K discloses operating properties and development projects across 17 countries and 21 named US markets, providing investors granular geographic segment data spanning Southern California, the San Francisco Bay Area, Seattle, Nashville, Atlanta, Houston, and others.

The DEF 14A reports specific operational metrics including 228 million square feet of leases signed in 2025, same store NOI of approximately $8.95 billion on 1,125 million square feet, and gross market capitalization of approximately $160.9 billion.

sections.title: Governance
sections.summary: Prologis' Board comprises 11 director nominees with 82% independence (9 of 11), and the company has adopted strong shareholder rights including annual director elections, majority voting, no rights plan, proxy access, and a 20% special meeting threshold. KPMG LLP serves as independent auditor with Audit Committee-recommended ratification for 2026.
points: Only Hamid Moghadam and Daniel Letter are non-independent directors, while the company maintains annual election of all directors since IPO, majority vote standard for uncontested elections, no stockholder rights plan, and proxy access with 3/3/20/20 standard.

The Board reduced the threshold for stockholders to call a special meeting to 20% of voting power in 2025, and the Governance Committee formally oversees Global Impact & Sustainability performance with a chief energy and sustainability officer on the management executive committee.

types.summary: Prologis operates approximately 1,125 million square feet of same store properties generating $8.95 billion in NOI, with a gross market capitalization of $160.9 billion that dwarfs most competitors. The company signed a record 228 million square feet of leases in 2025, demonstrating tenant demand at a scale few logistics landlords can match.

Operating properties across 17 countries and 21 US markets including Southern California, the San Francisco Bay Area, Seattle, New Jersey/New York City, and South Florida create a network where multinational tenants can access space in multiple logistics hubs through a single landlord. This geographic diversification spanning the US, Other Americas, Europe, and Asia increases tenant stickiness and reduces vacancy risk.

Prologis holds A2/A credit ratings from Moody's and S&P, enabling a diversified debt structure with maturities extending to 2054 and a cost of capital advantage over lower-rated peers. The Amended and Restated Global Senior Credit Agreement with Bank of America provides ongoing liquidity access that supports development pipelines and acquisitions through cycles.

With debt at 24.6% of gross market capitalization and $53.95 billion in stockholders' equity, Prologis operates eight unconsolidated co-investment ventures that deploy partner capital alongside its own, effectively expanding asset management reach without proportionate balance sheet expansion. The Strategic Capital segment manages vehicles like Prologis European Logistics Fund and China AMC Prologis Logistics REIT, creating fee streams that reward scale without requiring new property development.
